description:
An open-source alternative to Claude Cowork, powered by OpenCode

OpenWork is an extensible, open-source “Claude Work” style system for knowledge workers.
It’s a native desktop app that runs OpenCode under the hood, but presents it as a clean, guided workflow:
- pick a workspace
- start a run
- watch progress + plan updates
- approve permissions when needed
- reuse what works (templates + skills)
The goal: make “agentic work” feel like a product, not a terminal.
Highlights
- Preserve detached worker share credentials across restarts so reopened workers stay connected more reliably.
- Add a new Importing a skill guide and expand Slack integration docs with refreshed screenshots.
Fixes & polish
- Disable disconnected config providers in the app settings flow.
- Clarify Slack app connection requirements and clean up docs image naming.
Packaging / ops
- Bump OpenWork to v0.11.191 and refresh the AUR package metadata.
Compare:
https://github.com/different-ai/openwork/compare/v0.11.190...v0.11.191
